content.text:
Based on the zoomed-in view of atom N:10, I can clearly identify its neighbour atoms by following the covalent bonds (the connecting cylinders):

**Neighbour atoms of N:10:**

1. **C:4** - Carbon atom with index 4
2. **C:9** - Carbon atom with index 9

**geomconf_index:** 4056

Atom N:10 is covalently bonded to exactly two neighbouring atoms: C:4 on the left side (part of the bicyclic ring system) and C:9 on the right side (another carbon in the ring structure).

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 10, whose element should be N. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
